item.title: Blue Owl Private Credit Fund Raises $400 Million in Bond Market
item.description: A Blue Owl Capital Inc. private credit fund raised $400 million from bond investors Monday, marking the first deal of its kind in over a month, as worries about AI-disrupted software bets and looser lending standards have roiled the once red-hot industry.

item.title: UK’s Summer Gas Supply Looks Sufficient Despite War, Grid Says
item.description: Britain’s natural gas supply looks sufficient this summer despite the disruption to global energy flows caused by the Iran war, according to the grid manager.

item.title: Xi’s Petroyuan Dream Gets New Life From Trump’s War in Iran
item.description: The war in Iran is spurring a fresh wave of enthusiasm over prospects for China’s currency to more effectively rival the US dollar.

item.title: Singapore Interbank Rate Drops Toward Four-Year Low on Haven Bid
item.description: Singapore’s interbank rates are approaching four-year lows as haven demand due to the Iran war boosts inflows into the nation’s AAA rated assets.

item.title: Texas Man Charged With Attempted Murder in Altman Attack
item.description: Local and federal prosecutors filed attempted murder and other criminal charges against the man arrested Friday for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at the home of OpenAI Chief Executive Officer Sam Altman.

item.title: Stocks in Asia Poised to Climb on Iran Deal Hopes: Markets Wrap
item.description: Stocks in Asia were set to advance, tracking Wall Street gains as US President Donald Trump raised hopes for an Iran deal even as a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz took effect.

item.title: Gold Rises as Traders Weigh Potential to Revive US-Iran Talks
item.description: Gold edged higher after a two-day decline, as renewed optimism for a negotiated end to the US-Iran war eased concerns around inflation.

item.title: Raizen’s Creditors Said to Ask for 90% Equity Stake in Debt Swap
item.description: Raízen SA bondholders and bank creditors are asking for a stake of as much as 90% of the company in exchange for 45% of its debt in a restructuring, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Oracle Agrees to Buy Power From Bloom for AI Data Centers
item.description: Oracle Corp. agreed to purchase as much as 2.8 gigawatts of fuel-cell power from Bloom Energy Corp. to supply data centers for artificial intelligence work.

item.title: Oil Drops as US and Iran Weigh Meeting to Revive Ceasefire Talks
item.description: Oil fell as President Donald Trump said Tehran reached out over peace talks after the start of a US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Microsoft Raises Surface Prices Sharply in Face of Memory Crunch
item.description: Microsoft Corp. raised prices sharply across its Surface-branded device lineup, becoming the latest personal computer maker to pass along costs fueled by a historic memory chip shortage.

item.title: New Zealand Dairy Farmers Pocket Billions From Huge Capital Payout
item.description: New Zealand dairy farmers are about to get a record multi-billion dollar, tax-free payout. They may need every cent of it.

item.title: Thoma Bravo Winds Down Growth Equity to Focus on Owning Firms
item.description: Thoma Bravo is winding down its growth equity business less than five years after the software-focused investing firm debuted it, according to people familiar with the matter. Instead, it’s focusing more on its core buyouts strategy, which owns controlling interests in established firms.

item.title: Kraken Crypto Exchange Says Criminal Group Claims Access to Some Customer Data
item.description: Kraken, one of the world’s oldest crypto exchanges, said it’s being extorted by a criminal group that claims to have access to some client account information.

item.title: USDA Wants Farmers to Help With Fertilizer Probe as Prices Soar
item.description: The Trump administration is seeking information from farmers to help with an ongoing Justice Department probe into high costs for fertilizer, machinery and other farm inputs.

item.title: Colombia’s Valencia Vows $14 Billion Deficit Cut, Oil Push
item.description: Colombian presidential candidate Paloma Valencia is pledging to cut the nation’s budget deficit by 50 trillion pesos ($14 billion) and reinstate fiscal rules abandoned by the current administration, aiming to defuse what she describes as a ticking time bomb of unsustainable debt.

item.title: S&P 500 Erases Iran War-Driven Losses as Earnings Season Begins
item.description: The S&P 500 Index rallied to erase all of its losses since the start of the Iran war, as traders continue to bid up stocks since a temporary ceasefire agreement was announced and as US earnings season gets underway.

item.title: Google Faces Mass Arbitration by Advertisers Seeking Billions
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google is facing billions of dollars in potential damage claims as part of mass arbitration tied to the company’s online search and advertising technology businesses, which courts have ruled were illegal monopolies.

item.title: BlackRock Pivots Back to US Stocks With Iran War ‘Contained’
item.description: BlackRock strategists have returned to an overweight view on US stocks as they see the damage to global economic growth from the Middle East conflict “likely contained.”

item.title: Pakistan Is Looking at ‘All Options’ to Pay Back UAE Loan
item.description: Pakistan is considering financing from both countries and banks as options to repay a $3 billion loan from the United Arab Emirates and help maintain foreign-exchange reserve levels amid soaring oil prices, Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb said.

item.title: Nemetschek Strikes Deal for Thoma’s Software Firm HCSS
item.description: Nemetschek SE agreed to acquire construction management software provider Heavy Construction Systems Specialists from Thoma Bravo.

item.title: Zambia to Take $100 Million Hit to Counter Iran War Fuel Shock
item.description: Zambia will forgo about $100 million in revenue after suspending fuel taxes for three months to shield consumers from a surge in oil prices driven by the war in Iran, Finance Minister Situmbeko Musokotwane said, deepening strains on a budget already under pressure.

item.title: UN Warns of Food Risks From Fertilizer, Energy Trade Curbs
item.description: The United Nations urged countries not to limit shipments of energy and fertilizers as the conflict in the Middle East deepens, warning that such restrictions in the past have exacerbated spikes in global food prices.

item.title: Viking, Lone Pine Alumni Shutter $2 Billion Alua Hedge Fund
item.description: Alua Capital Management is closing its $2 billion hedge fund after about five years, concluding its returns were inadequate despite some periods of strong performance.

item.title: New York Fed Says Net Income Could Turn Positive This Year
item.description: Total net income from the Federal Reserve’s System Open Market Account is expected to turn positive in 2026, according to projections from the New York Fed released Monday.

item.title: One-Off Hits Threaten Confidence in Private Credit, Bailey Warns
item.description: Private credit funds are facing higher risks from one-off hits that could threaten confidence in the wider sector, Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey warned on Monday.

item.title: Villeroy Urges Vigilance as Poll Shows Firms Plan Higher Prices
item.description: Bank of France Governor Francois Villeroy de Galhau called for vigilance on inflation as the central bank’s monthly economic poll showed the share of firms lifting prices will double this month on the Iran war.

item.title: US Hospitals Are Emerging From Bankruptcy Only to Falter Again
item.description: The hospitals that emerged from the bankruptcies of Steward Health Care System and Prospect Medical Holdings Inc. were supposed to help stabilize one of the world’s largest hospital landlords by restoring a battered tenant base after two years of industry stress.

item.title: Sundheim’s D1 Hedge Fund Tumbled 6% in March as Biggest Stock Bets Soured
item.description: D1 Capital Partners’ equities book tumbled 6% in March, according to people familiar with the matter, making Dan Sundheim’s firm one of the worst-performing stock-pickers that month.

item.title: Austin Seeks $1 Billion of Munis for Airport Expansion Plan
item.description: Austin is set to bring a $1.18 billion airport revenue bond sale to market on Tuesday as growth in one of the nation’s fastest-expanding metro areas strains capacity at Austin-Bergstrom International Airport.
